_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Document and escalate incidents (including event’s history, status, and potential impact for further action) that may cause ongoing and immediate impact to the environment.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in triaging data pipeline incidents, including failure identification, impact assessment, escalation decision, and status communication to affected consumers.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-005 (RC, cosine: 0.419), DA-T-009 (DA, cosine: 0.401).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.3/10 (raw 0.528). Shared vocabulary: [impact, incidents, status]. DCWF-only terms [action, cause, document, environment, escalate, event] vs WIDAI-only terms [affected, assessment, communication, consumers, data, decision] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.875 (P=0.874, R=0.877). Balanced P/R — the 24-word DCWF element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.7%). "Document and escalate incidents (including event’s history, status, and potentia" and "Skill in triaging data pipeline incidents, including failure identification, imp" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.433 < STS 0.528 (gap -0.096). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [action, cause, document] and [affected, assessment, communication] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.1%. Shared tokens: [impact, incidents, status]. DCWF-only: [action, cause, document, environment, escalate, event]. WIDAI-only: [affected, assessment, communication, consumers, data, decision].
